name: Lone Wolf
description: USS Jacksonville alone in a 60x60 NM deep-water patrol box. Two Akulas are in there somewhere. Find them.
briefing:
CLASSIFICATION: SECRET

TO: Commanding Officer, USS Jacksonville (SSN-699)
FROM: COMSUBNORLANT
DTG: 090300Z NOV 1985

USS Jacksonville. Alone and unafraid.

SOSUS detected two contacts passing through the GIUK Gap 36 hours ago. Bearing tracks put them somewhere in your patrol box -- a 60-by-60 nautical mile square of deep Atlantic. They're deep, they're quiet, and they're not in a hurry.

You have Mk 48 torpedoes and patience. No surface support. No air cover. No second chances.

Find them. Kill them. Come home.

Intel assessment: both contacts are Akula-class. One may be running a racetrack pattern. The other has been inconsistent -- possible sprint-and-drift. Do not assume you have their position. You do not.

Standing orders: maintain below the thermal layer. Go active only if you have a firing solution. One ping, one kill.

The convergence zone is your friend. Use it before they do.
